workflow_rationale: Investigation revealed that all work described in the spec was completed in PR #6 (commit ce049af). This plan focuses on verification and documentation of the existing implementation.

phases.name: Verification
phases.description: Verify that all order queries use proper pagination and aggregation

subtasks.description: Verify dashboard uses groupBy for status counts

verification.instructions: Review app/account/page.tsx lines 37-50 to confirm groupBy() is used instead of fetching all orders and filtering in JavaScript

subtasks.notes: Verified app/account/page.tsx lines 37-50 correctly uses prisma.order.groupBy() for status counts. The implementation efficiently aggregates data in the database (grouping by status field with _count) rather than fetching all orders and filtering in JavaScript. This is the optimal pattern.

subtasks.description: Verify orders list page has pagination

verification.instructions: Review app/account/orders/page.tsx lines 35-45 to confirm pagination with take/skip and count() query. Check for ORDERS_PER_PAGE constant and pagination UI.

subtasks.notes: Verified app/account/orders/page.tsx has complete pagination implementation: ORDERS_PER_PAGE=10 constant (line 9), take/skip with parallel count() query (lines 35-45), and full pagination UI with Previous/Next buttons, page counter, and item count display (lines 133-168). This was implemented in subtask-2-1 of PR #6.

verification.instructions: Review app/account/invoices/page.tsx lines 35-52 to confirm pagination with take/skip and count() query. Check for ITEMS_PER_PAGE constant and pagination UI.

subtasks.notes: Verified app/account/invoices/page.tsx has complete pagination implementation: ITEMS_PER_PAGE=10 constant (line 9), take/skip with parallel count() query (lines 35-52), and full pagination UI with Previous/Next buttons, page counter, and item count display (lines 139-172). Pagination UI conditionally renders only when totalPages > 1. This was implemented in subtask-3-1 of PR #6.

verification.instructions: Review app/api/account/orders/route.ts to confirm pagination query params are accepted, validated, and used properly with take/skip

subtasks.notes: Verified app/api/account/orders/route.ts has complete pagination implementation: query params (page, limit) are parsed and validated (lines 13-15), skip/take calculated correctly (lines 18-19), prisma.order.count() used for total (lines 22-24), findMany() with skip/take (lines 27-46), and response includes full pagination metadata (lines 51-57). This was implemented in subtask-4-1 of PR #6.

verification.instructions: Review completion report documenting that all work was already done in PR #6

subtasks.notes: Create report documenting findings from investigation

acceptance_criteria: Confirm dashboard uses groupBy() for aggregation
Confirm orders page has pagination with take/skip
Confirm invoices page has pagination with take/skip
Confirm API route handles pagination params
verification_strategy.reasoning: Work already complete - verification only to confirm existing implementation matches spec requirements

subtasks_completed: subtask-2-1: Add pagination to orders list query
subtask-3-1: Add pagination to invoices list query
subtask-4-1: Add pagination to API route
